12.2: Ensure Cloudwatch Lambda insights is enabled
mappings.requirement_description: Ensure that Amazon CloudWatch Lambda Insights is enabled for your Amazon Lambda functions for enhanced monitoring.

mappings.requirement_context: 12.8: Ensure that Code Signing is enabled for Lambda functions.
mappings.requirement_description: Ensure that all your Amazon Lambda functions are configured to use the Code Signing feature in order to restrict the deployment of unverified code.

mappings.requirement_context: 12.12: Ensure encryption in transit is enabled for Lambda environment variables
mappings.requirement_description: As you can set your own environmental variables for Lambda it is important to also encrypt them for in transit protection.

mappings.requirement_context: 12.7: Ensure Lambda functions are referencing active execution roles.
mappings.requirement_description: In order to have the necessary permissions to access the AWS cloud services and resources Amazon Lambda functions should be associated with active(available) execution roles.

mappings.requirement_context: 12.9: Ensure there are no Lambda functions with admin privileges within your AWS account
mappings.requirement_description: Ensure that your Amazon Lambda functions don't have administrative permissions potentially giving the function access to all AWS cloud services and resources.

mappings.requirement_description: Always using a recent version of the execution environment configured for your Amazon Lambda functions adheres to best practices for the newest software features, the latest security patches and bug fixes, and performance and reliability.

mappings.requirement_context: 12.5: Ensure every Lambda function has its own IAM Role
mappings.requirement_description: Every Lambda function should have a one to one IAM execution role and the roles should not be shared between functions.
